100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 19, 1926, the British government faced escalating tensions as workers in the coal industry prepared for a nationwide strike, demanding better wages and working conditions. In the United States, the aftermath of the recent flooding in West Virginia continued to challenge recovery efforts, with communities struggling to rebuild. Meanwhile, in Germany, the political landscape grew increasingly unstable as radical factions intensified their activities in the wake of economic turmoil.
